summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering several topics including a presentation by County Engineer Jeremy Evans on a proposed roundabout at the Woodville Pike and Deerfield Road intersection, an update on the township's new digital gateway sign, the purchase of a replacement lawn mower, and an extension of the tornado-related zoning moratorium. The board also entered executive session to discuss personnel matters and approved a suspension of an officer.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (August 8-11, 2023) – passed
Motion to increase appropriations for service vehicles/equipment by $12,710.06 – passed
Motion to purchase 2023 Gravely lawn mower from Koenig Equipment not to exceed $12,710.06 from ARPA funds – passed
Motion to declare surplus/obsolete and sell 2002 X-Mark mower on GovDeals – passed
Motion to revise indigent burial of Janice Lay to $853 payable to Advantage Cremation – passed
Motion to extend tornado zoning moratorium (Resolution 17-2023) through June 30, 2024 – passed
Motion to vacate portion of Mustang Circle (Resolution R01-2023) – passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Officer Matt Richwine to attend less lethal instructor course (October 18-20, 2023, $895 registration) – passed
Motion to set Trick or Treat for Tuesday, October 31, 2023, 6-8 PM – passed
Motion to adjourn to executive session at 7:06 PM – passed
Motion to suspend Officer Reporter Field for two shifts totaling 24 hours – passed
Motion to adjourn meeting at 8:11 PM – passed
action_items: County Engineer Jeremy Evans to provide roundabout statistics from Butler County and additional information to Administrator Rose before next meeting
Justin from One Stop Signs to work with Bob and Chad on raising the secondary gateway sign and improving its visibility and content
Administrator Rose to research previous board vote on permanent zoning change allowing camper/RV dwelling and get it to zoning consultant Liz for potential zoning amendment
Lisa to coordinate healthcare committee forms and follow up on options discussed at committee meeting
Chief Myers to schedule a special meeting for fire station 18 cost presentation and funding discussion
Staff to cross-train on digital sign software (Stacks) and set up multiple user logins
One Stop Signs staff to power up the digital sign tomorrow and assist with PC connection setup
key_amounts: $12,710.06 for 2023 Gravely lawn mower from Koenig Equipment (ARPA funds)
$271,000 estimated design cost for Woodville Pike/Deerfield Road roundabout
$100,000 local share of design cost (proposed split $50,000 each between Goshen and Miami townships)
$500,000 local construction match for roundabout (80% federally funded)
$895 registration cost for Officer Richwine's less lethal instructor course
$853 revised indigent burial cost for Janice Lay (up $50 from previously approved $803)
notable_topics: Woodville Pike and Deerfield Road roundabout project presentation by County Engineer Jeremy Evans
Gateway digital sign update and visibility concerns
Purchase of replacement lawn mower using ARPA funds
Extension of tornado zoning moratorium allowing temporary RV/camper dwelling through June 2024
Vacating portion of Mustang Circle in Villages of Belmont
Recognition of Service Director Chad Meadows for saving a 13-year-old football player in cardiac arrest
Commendation of Goshen PD officers Alcorn, Groves, and Collier for school community engagement
Fire station 18 architectural update and Medic 19 ambulance replacement (10-month timeline)
Go-to Road bridge reopening expected next day
Trick or treat date set for October 31, 2023
Personnel discipline matter in executive session
Passing of Pete Schmidt (Rep. Jean Schmidt's husband)
attendees: Chad Meadows (Service Director, absent - at award ceremony)
notes: Service Director Chad Meadows was absent because he was receiving an award from the Little Miami School Board for performing CPR and using an AED to save a 13-year-old football player who went into cardiac arrest three times during practice. The board also referenced a fatal school bus crash that morning at Northwestern High School in Clark County during the invocation. The roundabout project discussion revealed financial strain on the township due to competing priorities including tornado recovery and fire station replacement. An executive session was held from approximately 7:06 PM to 8:10 PM regarding personnel matters.
title: BOT 8-22-23
